meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics for Founders: Solve 'Can't Finish' Loops (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop the endless build loop. Use this advanced analytics setup guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to validate features, kill bloat, and ship revenue.
content.answer_block: You can't finish projects because you are building for ghosts, not users. By integrating event-driven analytics into your MVP, you gain objective proof of what matters. Replace 'gut feeling' with telemetry to identify high-value features, kill dead code, and focus your engineering efforts on the exact path to revenue.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with 'perfect' products that nobody actually uses. You're trapped in a cycle of infinite polish because you lack the objective feedback loop required to define 'done.' Without data, every feature is a guess, and every guess is a potential distraction from your primary goal: monetization.

True velocity doesn't come from coding faster; it comes from knowing exactly what not to build. By instrumenting your application with surgical precision, you transform your build process from a blind walk in the dark into a data-driven sprint. It is time to stop guessing and start measuring.

steps.title: Define North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, define the three events that correlate with user success. If the user doesn't hit these, your product fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS business model and suggest 5 high-signal event triggers for PostHog or Mixpanel that correlate directly to subscription conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate an event tracking schema based on my current project architecture and business model.'

steps.title: Automated Instrumentation Layer
steps.description: Inject a centralized analytics provider that captures state changes without cluttering your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current event tracking to use a singleton analytics wrapper that supports batching and async processing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create an AnalyticsService class and inject it into the core user session flow.'

steps.title: User Journey Funnel Mapping
steps.description: Identify the drop-off points in your onboarding. If users leave, they aren't 'finishing'—the product is broken.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a funnel analysis prompt for my analytics dashboard to identify friction in the user signup to 'First Value' path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify all major entry/exit points in the UI and add tracking events to those components.'

steps.title: Feature Usage Heatmaps
steps.description: Track which features receive zero interaction to prune the codebase and reduce maintenance overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to analyze feature usage data and flag components with less than 2% engagement for potential deprecation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add usage tracking to all components within the /features directory.'

steps.title: Conversion Loop Closing
steps.description: Tie analytics events directly to your Stripe/revenue metrics to see exactly which features lead to paid upgrades.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard query that joins my Stripe customer data with in-app engagement events to calculate Feature-to-Revenue conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement a hook that logs subscription events alongside the last 5 user actions taken.'

pro_tips: Track 'Intent' events, not just 'Action' events; knowing why they clicked is more valuable than knowing they did.
Implement 'Feature Flags' with your analytics to A/B test features before you fully commit to building them out.
Use 'Anonymous ID' mapping to track user behavior before they sign up to understand the pre-conversion journey.
Automate your analytics reporting to Slack; if you don't look at the data daily, it might as well not exist.
Aggressively delete code for features that have under 1% usage; bloat is the primary cause of project stall.
content.next_steps: Once your dashboard is live, spend one week observing user behavior without changing a single line of code. Identify the top three friction points and use Claude Code to refactor only those specific areas.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products that users actually buy. The difference between a stalled project and a profitable SaaS is the data you collect today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
